inputs.creative_direction: Technology teardown of over-ear wireless noise-canceling headphones. The headphones deconstruct symmetrically, revealing internal engineering layer by layer. Each component separates as a solid piece -- never as particles or debris. LAYERS separate outward from both earcups simultaneously in this order: LAYER 1 (outermost, lifts first): both outer ear cup shells detach as two solid curved panels, drifting outward left and right to expose the internals beneath. The matte plastic catches pink-blue neon light on newly exposed inner surfaces. LAYER 2: both ear pad cushions (memory foam wrapped in protein leather) peel away as two solid oval rings, floating gently outward, revealing the driver baffle behind them. LAYER 3: the 40mm neodymium dynamic driver units separate from each earcup -- two flat disc assemblies with visible copper voice coils and diaphragm membranes, drifting apart while catching metallic reflections. LAYER 4: the ANC system -- a thin PCB board from each earcup floats out showing the Bluetooth SoC chip, DAC, amplifier IC, and ANC processor. External and internal MEMS microphones (tiny silver dots) remain attached to each board. A flat lithium-polymer battery cell separates from one earcup as a distinct silver pouch. LAYER 5: the bare headband skeleton remains in center -- the stainless steel slider rails, adjustment click mechanism, and internal ribbon cable running through the headband arch are now fully visible. Every layer is a solid component or assembled module. PACING: layers separate during the first 55% of the animation. The final 45% holds ALL components visibly floating at their separated positions, drifting very slowly apart, giving the viewer time to see the full internal architecture. Do NOT end while layers are still separating. CAMERA: starts front-facing, then slowly orbits 20-25 degrees to a 3/4 angle during the hold phase, revealing depth between floating layers. The existing pink-to-blue neon gradient backdrop remains throughout, each newly exposed surface catching the gradient light with metallic and copper highlights.

notes: Run 2: technology-focused teardown. 5 layers: outer shells, ear pads, 40mm drivers, ANC electronics + battery, headband skeleton. Same reference image as run 1. Upload reference to get URL if needed.
